reasoning: I'll provide a concise, neutral summary of the article, capturing the main developments, stakeholders' positions, and background context.
output: Pakistan's regulator says the ban on YouTube is no longer necessary after Google launched a Pakistan-specific version, and all internet service providers have been told to restore access. The government states that blocking requests can be sent to Google directly, and Google/YouTube will restrict access to offending material for Pakistani users after review. YouTube, however, says government removal requests are not automatically granted and will be recorded in its Transparency Report; videos that violate community guidelines may be removed. Some activists call for greater transparency about any deal with Google, while many Pakistanis welcomed the return of YouTube. The move comes after the 2012 Supreme Court ban linked to the Innocence of Muslims video, and blasphemy remains a sensitive issue in the country. Google has also launched local YouTube versions for Nepal, Pakistan and Sri Lanka.
